title: Castle in the Air - Chapter Five: Which tells how Flower-in-the-Night’s father wished to raise Abdullah above all others in the land (Fragment 2)
text:
“Back to the booth!” he whispered to it. “Quickly! Please!”

This time the carpet obeyed, as quickly as it had the night before. It was up off the bank in an eye blink and then hurtling sideways across a forbiddingly high wall. Abdullah had just a glimpse of a large party of northern mercenaries milling around in the lamplit garden before he was speeding above the sleeping roofs and moonlit towers of Zanzib. He had barely time to reflect that Flower-in-the-Night’s father must be even richer than he had thought—few people could afford that many hired soldiers, and mercenaries from the north were the most expensive kind—before the carpet planed downward and brought him smoothly in through the curtains to the middle of his booth.

There he gave himself up to despair.

A djinn had stolen Flower-in-the-Night and the carpet had refused to follow. He knew that was not surprising. A djinn, as everyone in Zanzib knew, commanded enormous powers in the air and the earth. No doubt the djinn had, as a precaution, ordered everything in the garden to stay where it was while he carried Flower-in-the-Night away. It had probably not even noticed the carpet, or Abdullah on it, but the carpet’s lesser magic had been forced to give way to the djinn’s command. So the djinn had stolen away Flower-in-the-Night, whom Abdullah loved more than his own soul, just at the moment when she was about to run into his arms, and there seemed nothing he could do.

He wept.

After that he vowed to throw away all the money hidden in his clothes. It was useless to him now. But before he did, he gave himself over to grief again, noisy misery at first, in which he lamented out loud and beat his breast in the manner of Zanzib; then, as cocks crowed and people began moving about, he fell into silent despair. There was no point even in moving. Other people might bustle about and whistle and clank buckets, but Abdullah was no longer part of that life. He stayed crouching on the magic carpet, wishing he were dead.

So miserable was he that it never occurred to him that he might be in any danger himself. He paid no attention when all the noises in the Bazaar stopped, like birds when a hunter enters a wood. He did not really notice the heavy marching of feet or the regular clank-clank-clank of mercenary armor that went with it. When someone barked “ Halt !” outside his booth, he did not even turn his head. But he did turn around when the curtains of the booth were torn down. He was sluggishly surprised. He blinked his swollen eyes against the powerful sunlight and wondered vaguely what a troop of northern soldiers was doing coming in here.

“That’s him,” said someone in civilian clothes, who might have been Hakim, and then faded prudently away before Abdullah’s eyes could focus on him.

“You!” snapped the squad leader. “Out. With us.”

“What?” said Abdullah.

“Fetch him,” said the leader.

Abdullah was bewildered. He protested feebly when they dragged him to his feet and twisted his arms to make him walk. He went on protesting as they marched him at the double—clank-clank, clank-clank—out of the Bazaar and into the West Quarter. Before long he was protesting very strongly indeed. “What is this?” he panted. “I demand … as a citizen … where we are … going!”

“Shut up. You’ll see,” they answered. They were too fit to pant.

A short while after, they ran Abdullah in under a massive gate made of blocks of stone that glared white in the sun, into a blazing courtyard, where they spent five minutes outside an ovenlike smithy loading Abdullah with chains. He protested even more. “What is this for? Where is this? I demand to know!”

meta.author: Diana Wynne Jones
meta.sectionTitle: Chapter Five: Which tells how Flower-in-the-Night’s father wished to raise Abdullah above all others in the land

vocabulary.definition: To have enough money or resources to be able to do or buy something.
vocabulary.example: Few people could afford that many hired soldiers.

vocabulary.definition: Confused and puzzled completely.
vocabulary.example: Abdullah was bewildered.

vocabulary.definition: Very hot or bright like a blazing fire or sun.
vocabulary.example: Into a blazing courtyard.

vocabulary.definition: Metal links connected together, used to restrain or confine.
vocabulary.example: Loading Abdullah with chains.

vocabulary.definition: A person not in the armed services or police.
vocabulary.example: Someone in civilian clothes.

vocabulary.definition: To have control over or authority to direct.
vocabulary.example: A djinn commanded enormous powers in the air and the earth.

vocabulary.definition: Bending the body low by bringing the knees up towards the chest.
vocabulary.example: He stayed crouching on the magic carpet.

vocabulary.definition: To ask authoritatively or forcefully for something.
vocabulary.example: He demanded to know where they were going.

vocabulary.definition: The complete loss or absence of hope.
vocabulary.example: He gave himself up to despair.

vocabulary.definition: A supernatural being in Arab folklore, capable of influencing events.
vocabulary.example: A djinn had stolen Flower-in-the-Night.

vocabulary.definition: In good physical condition.
vocabulary.example: They were too fit to pant.

vocabulary.definition: In a way that makes something seem difficult or dangerous to do or approach.
vocabulary.example: A forbiddingly high wall.

vocabulary.definition: Moving swiftly and with great force.
vocabulary.example: Hurtling sideways across a forbiddingly high wall.

vocabulary.definition: To express grief or sorrow about something.
vocabulary.example: He lamented out loud and beat his breast.

vocabulary.definition: Illuminated by a lamp.
vocabulary.example: Northern mercenaries milling around in the lamplit garden.

vocabulary.definition: Of less importance or smaller in size, amount, or degree.
vocabulary.example: The carpet’s lesser magic had been forced to give way.

vocabulary.definition: Walking in a deliberate, regular way, often as a group.
vocabulary.example: He did not notice the heavy marching of feet.

vocabulary.definition: Professional soldiers hired to serve in a foreign army.
vocabulary.example: A large party of northern mercenaries milling around in the lamplit garden.

vocabulary.definition: Very unhappy or uncomfortable.
vocabulary.example: So miserable was he that it never occurred to him that he might be in danger.

vocabulary.definition: To comply with the command, direction, or request of a person or law.
vocabulary.example: The carpet obeyed, as quickly as it had the night before.

vocabulary.definition: To move smoothly and continuously through the air, like a glider.
vocabulary.example: The carpet planed downward and brought him smoothly in through the curtains.

vocabulary.definition: An action taken in advance to prevent harm or secure good results.
vocabulary.example: As a precaution, ordered everything in the garden to stay where it was.

vocabulary.definition: To express an objection or disapproval.
vocabulary.example: He protested feebly when they dragged him to his feet.

vocabulary.definition: To think deeply or carefully about something.
vocabulary.example: He had barely time to reflect that Flower-in-the-Night’s father must be even richer than he had thought.

vocabulary.definition: A workshop where metal is shaped or forged.
vocabulary.example: Outside an ovenlike smithy.

vocabulary.definition: To speak abruptly or sharply.
vocabulary.example: “You!” snapped the squad leader.

vocabulary.definition: A small group of soldiers or police officers forming a unit.
vocabulary.example: The squad leader.

vocabulary.definition: A group of soldiers or people acting together.
vocabulary.example: A troop of northern soldiers.

vocabulary.definition: To solemnly promise to do something.
vocabulary.example: He vowed to throw away all the money.

vocabulary.definition: To speak very softly using one's breath rather than one's throat, especially for the sake of privacy.
vocabulary.example: He whispered to it.
